key_points: New MASH dorm on Mill St gets strong praise for quality
Old dorms demolished; deferred maintenance was a serious problem
Summer lease traps are a recurring student complaint
key_quotes: A huge step up from Best Hall for sure!
The amount of deferred maintenance around the campus is staggering. They don't do proper maintenance on anything.
Most people who need a place to stay for the summer usually get something set with the school lol. Don't sign these leases if you aren't okay with having it over the Summer

key_points: Party school reputation fading but Greek life still active
Hope Church aggressively recruits freshmen — widely called a cult
249 people arrested in single night for underage drinking at Tempe bar
key_quotes: Don't engage with Hope Church — they're relentless if they get your contact info
I don't know what specifically happened, but it is crazy to see how much ASU has changed regarding its reputation as a party school
Joining a fraternity gave me a close group in a huge school — more about friendships than constant parties

key_points: MU lunch lines are brutal 12–2pm; plan around them or starve
Aramark dining workers voted to strike over wages and conditions
Chick-fil-A on campus is perpetually drowning in orders
key_quotes: Best to go before noon so like 11am or shortly after 1:30pm for reasonable waits.
It is insane the shit that goes on at the food court... My last year, I was tasked with training people they shouldn't have hired.
if i had a magic wand, i would bring taco bell back

key_points: Burnout is near-universal among students juggling heavy coursework
Post-graduation depression reported as surprisingly common
Students self-organize support online but institutional support rarely mentioned
key_quotes: You really don't [have free time]. It's a never ending cycle of grinding, recovering during the breaks, rinse and repeat.
I had the biggest crisis of my life right after graduation. Your whole life has been about school until this point.
Do your self a favor and make sure you're exercising each day. And try to take at least one real day off a week.

key_points: Out-of-state cost repeatedly flagged as not worth it without full coverage
Textbooks cost extra — $400+ is common, not covered by tuition
Starbucks SCAP program a legitimate pathway for working adults
key_quotes: If you are a non resident make sure the cost is 100% covered because it's extremely not worth the price if you are actually paying the out of state cost.
$400 will get you one engineering textbook.
Don't forget you get charged a non-optional extra fee to use the gym.

key_points: Session A/B online courses feel like cramming, not learning
Cheating widespread in large in-person math classes
Star professors leave and the loss is felt hard by students
key_quotes: Feels like I'm not really learning just grabbing and dumping for the test.
discussion board is just chatgpt talking to chatgpt
Dr Dave is a great teacher and really cares about his students... whoever replaces him has great shoes to fill.

key_points: SCAI/engineering advisors described as rigid, slow, and unhelpful
University held student's job offer hostage over 0.33-credit rounding error
President Crow consulting Trump admin and cutting DEI programs angers many
key_quotes: Don't transfer to ASU, the SCAI is nothing but a bunch of money grabbing assholes.
In my experience the advisors were some of the most rigid and unhelpful people I have ever met.
So they will cut ties with an organization that actually helps students but won't cut ties with professors who were in the Epstein files?

key_points: Tempe heat is brutal — hydration PSAs are a semester tradition
Campus is genuinely photogenic with diverse architecture and stunning sunsets
Parking enforcement is aggressive and garages are overcrowded
key_quotes: I cannot even begin to imagine having to walk through campus in this climate right now — 105-110F is absolutely VILE.
The scattered architecture from 20s to 50s onto 00s makes it a great landscape, and nothing beats an AZ sunset.
If you park like this in a packed garage, I hope you get explosive diarrhea mid exam.

key_points: ASU split from Handshake — students skeptical of replacement platform
CS job market warnings appear repeatedly in student posts
Internship culture is active; students land competitive roles with 3.9 GPAs
key_quotes: I've applied to at least a hundred jobs and internships on Handshake and maybe 5 ever actually got back to me.
First off congrats, but are you sure you wanna stick with computer science? The job market ain't looking too good for us CS majors rn.
The amount of fake job postings on there was crazy.

key_points: In-state or scholarship students find solid value; out-of-state sticker price is a red flag
Gym access requires extra mandatory fees on top of tuition
Online degrees praised for working adults but feel transactional to traditional students
key_quotes: If you are a non resident make sure the cost is 100% covered because it's extremely not worth the price.
Call me privileged but we don't pay all that tuition to have to resort to using a third of the gym for a week straight.
3 grand for lectures made with notebook LM podcasts. I love college.

key_points: Massive size means you create your own experience — community not automatic
Strong culture of nontraditional and working adult students who genuinely value ASU
Sports energy is real when it shows up; fan culture improving
key_quotes: The reason why ASU is so good is because it can be literally anything you want it to be — you just have to be willing to go get it.
ASU gave me a shot to improve myself and learn and grow in my 30s and I'm forever grateful.
I know ASU often ends up as the butt of the jokes, but I'm happy to see opportunities exist outside T20 schools.

key_points: **Administration:** Epstein foundation donated $2M+ to ASU; named professors in files still employed
**Cults:** Hope Church aggressively targets freshmen every fall with deceptive recruitment tactics
**AI/Integrity:** ASU using AI to chop professor lectures without consent; discussion boards are bots talking to bots
key_quotes: So they will cut ties with an organization that helps students but won't cut ties with professors in the Epstein files?
When I was a freshman my roommate made the mistake of talking to them and one Sunday they were banging on our dorm room door to go to church.
I beta tested it and gave tons of feedback on how bad it was... the content it puts together makes no logical sense.

key_points: Barrett Honors College gives a small-school feel inside a massive university
Chrome extension integrates RateMyProfessor directly into class search — student-built
Professors occasionally step up big: one answered physics finals questions publicly when Canvas crashed
key_quotes: I built a chrome extension to show Rate My Professor ratings directly in the ASU class search — already used by 160+ students.
If it would help, I will attempt to answer as many questions as I possibly can for physics finals. I teach PHY 100 level and have my PhD.
Opportunities exist outside T20 schools — Barrett Honors College is one of them.
